visual_checks: Identify all prerecorded video content and determine if visual-only information is present that is not described in the audio track
Look for an audio description toggle or alternative audio track in the video player
Check if there is a separate version of the video with audio descriptions linked nearby
Assess scenes where actions, settings, facial expressions, or on-screen text are shown without verbal description
Determine if the video content can be fully understood by listening to the audio alone
pass_conditions: Audio description is provided for all prerecorded video content in synchronized media
Audio descriptions narrate important visual information during natural pauses in dialogue
On-screen text (titles, credits, signs) is read aloud in the audio description
Key visual actions and scene changes are described
Audio description track is available via player controls or a linked alternative version
fail_conditions: Prerecorded video with visual-only information has no audio description available
Audio description is provided but misses critical visual content needed for understanding
Only a text transcript is provided instead of audio description (text alternative only satisfies 1.2.3, not 1.2.5 at AA)
Audio description link or toggle is present but non-functional
na_conditions: Page contains no prerecorded synchronized media
The audio track already describes all important visual content — no additional audio description is needed
Video content is audio-only or video-only (not synchronized media)
ne_conditions: AI response could not be parsed
Video could not be played to assess whether audio description is present
Cannot determine visual content from screenshots alone to verify audio description completeness
common_mistakes: Assuming a text transcript satisfies this criterion — at AA level, actual audio description is required, not just a text alternative
Audio description that describes only trivial visual details while missing plot-critical actions
Audio description that talks over dialogue, making both unintelligible
Providing audio description for some videos on the site but not all

examples.pass: A documentary video has an audio description track that narrates 'A bulldozer pushes rubble into a growing pile beside the damaged building' during scenes shown without commentary
examples.fail: An instructional video shows step-by-step assembly of furniture visually but the narration only says 'as you can see here' without describing what is shown, and no audio description track exists
examples.partial: Audio description is available for the main content but on-screen text overlays (names, locations, statistics) are not read aloud

plain_meaning: At Level AA, prerecorded synchronized media must provide audio description of the video content. Unlike Level A (1.2.3), a text alternative alone is NOT sufficient here -- an actual audio description track is required. The intent is that blind and low-vision users listening to the content hear narration describing key visuals in the gaps of the dialogue. Media that is a media alternative for text on the same page and is clearly labeled as such is exempt.
scope_applies_to: Prerecorded synchronized media where visual content carries information not conveyed by the primary audio
Video lectures, demonstrations, product walkthroughs with meaningful visuals
Embedded third-party video players hosting such content
Self-hosted <video> with an audio track conveying primary content
scope_does_not_apply_to: Live content -- no audio description required at AA for live media
Prerecorded audio-only or video-only content -- that belongs to SC 1.2.1
Captions for the audio track -- that belongs to SC 1.2.2
Extended audio description -- that belongs to SC 1.2.7 (AAA)
Media that is a media alternative for text on the same page and is clearly labeled as such
Video where the audio already conveys all visual information (pure talking-head with non-informational visuals)
Keyboard player operability -- that belongs to SC 2.1.1
Sign language interpretation -- that belongs to SC 1.2.6 (AAA)
borderline_calibration: At Level AA, a text transcript alone does NOT satisfy 1.2.5. An audio description track is required.
If the visuals are genuinely non-informational (talking head with static background), mark not-applicable and justify.
Audio description can be in the main track or a separate selectable track; both satisfy.
Missing audio description on visually-informational prerecorded video is a hard failure at medium+.
